title: Sweet and Sour Salmon
description: A simple Chinese-inspired dish with salmon, sweet and sour flavors, and a crispy texture.
instructions: Season the salmon fillets with salt and pepper.
In a shallow dish, mix together the cornstarch and a pinch of salt.
Dip each salmon fillet into the cornstarch mixture, coating both sides evenly.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the coated salmon fillets to the skillet and sear for 2-3 minutes on each side, until golden brown.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 8-10 minutes, until the salmon is cooked through.
Meanwhile, heat the sweet chili sauce and soy sauce in a small saucepan over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and bell pepper to the saucepan and cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
Stir in the vinegar and sugar, and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, until the sauce has thickened slightly.
Serve the salmon fillets with the sweet and sour sauce spooned over the top.
